title: cupaniopsis tomentella
intro: Cupaniopsis tomentella, also known as the hairy Cupaniopsis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is a small to medium-sized tree native to Australia, with potential medicinal properties due to its bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Cupaniopsis tomentella include infusions and extracts, which can be consumed as teas or added to topical creams and ointments for their medicinal benefits.
